title: What Heavenly Smiles! O Lady Mine
content:
What heavenly smiles! O Lady mine
 Through my very heart they shine;
 And, if my brow gives back their light,
 Do thou look gladly on the sight;
 As the clear Moon with modest pride
 Beholds her own bright beams
 Reflected from the mountain's side
 And from the headlong streams.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Wordsworth, titled "What Heavenly Smiles! O Lady Mine", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
